title: Working Tree, Index, Commits, and Atomic History · Repository Trace: Three Tree Model
summary: Turn a mixed set of feature, formatting, and accidental changes into reviewable commits without losing work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git three tree model increment in an atomic commit construction workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.
Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Three Tree Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Three Tree Model contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace head, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.

content.text: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Three Tree Model condition decided your prediction.

steps.title: Bound Three Tree Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Three Tree Model application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace head, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.

content.text: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: README.md has one intended line, data/incidents.csv has an accidental edit, and evidence.txt is untracked. Construct one atomic commit by comparing working tree, index, and HEAD, then verify the staged diff. A defensible solution applies Three Tree Model, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an atomic commit construction workbook.

content.code:
git status --short --branch
git diff -- README.md
git add --patch README.md
git diff --staged --check
git commit -m 'Document verified status behavior'
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: git add sends files directly from the working tree into permanent history. This fails the Three Tree Model contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Three Tree Model evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-three-tree-model-1as95v" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Three Tree Model into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-three-tree-model-1as95v
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace HEAD, index, and working-tree versions of a path before using status, diff, add, restore, or commit.
